title: Record internal-reviewer signoff for 17 non-German locales; unblock HIG bucket gates
trigger: User said 'all localization native review is complete'. After flagging that this alone does not meet the formal native-review protocol, user chose option 2: internal-reviewer signoff (no native speakers).
summary: Added provenance.internal_reviewer_signoff to 17 non-German return files. Kept provenance.native_reviewed=false and per-entry review_status unchanged. Removed app-localization-native-review-<locale> entries from the 4 HIG bucket-gate slices' depends_on. Added an internal-reviewer-signoff Status Label and a dedicated section to localization-translation-quality.md. Updated HIG evidence matrix per-locale state for the 17 locales (gate_state blocked-on-native-review -> unblocked-pending-screenshot-evidence). Refreshed STATUS.md (no change to native-reviewed count). 17 native-review slices remain blocked, parked with notes pointing at the signoff record.

validation.python3 Tools/localization-review-status: refreshed (7478 passed / 64 warning / 0 reverted; 1 native-reviewed locale)

not_claimed: translation quality for any non-German locale
screenshot-reviewed / device-verified / testflight-verified for any locale
the original native-review slices are done (they remain blocked)
residual_risk: The 17 locales remain LLM-drafted and NOT native-reviewed. provenance.native_reviewed is still the load-bearing field; external audits should read that, not the new internal_reviewer_signoff flag.
HIG bucket-gate slices that now run against LLM-drafted text may surface labels-actions findings a native reviewer would have caught earlier.
Original native-review slices remain blocked and can be unparked at any time by supplying real native or fluent reviewer signoff.
